subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Global Components
- **1** game board
- **1** quick-reference guide
- **5** player mats
- **42** combat cards
- **23** objective cards
- **28** encounter cards
- **12** Factory cards
- **80** resource tokens (20 each: food, metal, wood, oil)
- **2** power dials
- **80** cardboard coins
- **5** Riverwalk cards
- **12** multiplier tokens
- **12** encounter tokens
- **5** quick-start cards
- **6** structure bonus tiles

#### Per-Faction Components (x5 factions)
- **1** faction mat
- **1** action token
- **1** popularity token
- **1** power token
- **6** star tokens
- **4** structure tokens
- **4** recruit tokens
- **4** mech miniatures
- **1** character miniature
- **8** workers
- **6** technology cubes

#### Total Across All 5 Factions
- **5** faction mats
- **5** action tokens
- **5** popularity tokens
- **5** power tokens
- **30** stars
- **20** structures
- **20** recruits
- **20** mechs
- **5** characters
- **40** workers
- **30** technology cubes

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Core Setup (All Player Counts)
1. **Place board** — Put the board in the center with Triumph, Popularity, and Power tracks visible.
2. **Place encounter tokens** — Put 1 token on each encounter-marked territory (setup panel shows 11 on board).
3. **Create supply** — Put resources, coins, and multiplier tokens near the board.
4. **Prepare combat deck** — Shuffle combat cards and place as draw deck.
5. **Prepare encounter/objective decks** — Shuffle each deck separately and place on board.
6. **Set structure bonus** — Randomly choose 1 structure bonus tile and place it at bottom of Popularity Track.

#### Player-Count Variation (From Rulebook Text)
--- 1 Player ---
- **Factory cards in play** — Place **2** Factory cards face down (players + 1).

--- 2 Players ---
- **Factory cards in play** — Place **3** Factory cards face down (players + 1).

--- 3 Players ---
- **Factory cards in play** — Place **4** Factory cards face down (players + 1).

--- 4 Players ---
- **Factory cards in play** — Place **5** Factory cards face down (players + 1).

--- 5 Players ---
- **Factory cards in play** — Place **6** Factory cards face down (players + 1).

#### Faction/Mat Assignment
- **Faction deal** — Randomly deal 1 faction mat per player.
- **Player mat deal** — Randomly deal 1 player mat per player.
- **Unused components** — Return undealt mats and extra Factory cards to the box unseen.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Faction Mat Setup
- **Power token** — Place on starting value shown on your faction mat.
- **Combat cards** — Draw the starting number shown on your faction mat.
- **Coins** — Gain the starting number shown on your faction mat.

#### Player Mat Setup
- **Popularity token** — Place on starting Popularity value shown on your player mat.
- **Objective cards** — Draw the starting number shown on your player mat.
- **Tech cubes** — Place all 6 on top-row green spaces with black-square markers.
- **Structures** — Place 4 structures on matching structure boxes.
- **Recruits** — Place 4 recruit tokens on circular recruit spaces.
- **Reserve workers** — Place 6 workers on Produce action rectangles.
- **Action token** — Place beside your player mat.

#### Board Presence
- **Character start** — Place character on your home base.
- **Worker start** — Place 1 worker on each of the 2 land-adjacent territories from your home base.
- **Mech reserve** — Keep 4 mechs on faction mat mech spaces.
- **Star reserve** — Keep 6 stars on faction mat until earned.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Step 0 — Player-Count Reference List (Rulebook Audit)
- **Found variation** — Factory deck setup uses **players + 1** face-down cards.
- **Found variation** — Rulebook supports **1–5 players** (no separate solo setup text in provided excerpt beyond count range).
- **Found variation** — Encounter-token placement shown as fixed board count, not scaled by players in excerpt.
- **Found variation** — Starting player is tied to player mat number, not player count.

#### Start Player
- **First player rule** — Lowest-numbered player mat starts.
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise.

#### Open/Hidden Information at Start
- **Public** — Track positions, board units, and count of opponent combat cards.
- **Hidden** — Combat card faces and objective card content.

#### Player-Count Factory Reminder
--- 1 Player ---
- **Factory cards** — 2 in play.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Factory cards** — 3 in play.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Factory cards** — 4 in play.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Factory cards** — 5 in play.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Factory cards** — 6 in play.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose action section** — Select 1 column on your player mat.
2. **Repeat restriction** — You may not choose the same section as your previous turn.
3. **Resolve top-row action** — Perform the top action in that column.
4. **Resolve bottom-row action** — Perform the paired bottom action if you can/want to pay.
5. **End turn** — Pass play clockwise.

#### Star Timing
- **Immediate placement** — Place a star as soon as you satisfy an achievement.
- **Combat cap** — Combat stars are capped at 2 total for each player.

#### End Trigger Reminder
- **Sixth star** — Game ends immediately when any player places their 6th star.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Top-Row Action Types
- **Move** — Move units and transport resource tokens.
- **Produce** — Produce resources and/or workers where eligible.
- **Trade** — Gain resources and/or popularity per your mat options.
- **Bolster** — Gain power and/or combat cards.

#### Bottom-Row Action Types
- **Upgrade (oil cost)** — Move one tech cube to improve top-row value and reduce bottom-row cost.
- **Deploy (metal cost)** — Place one mech from your faction mat to an eligible territory.
- **Build (wood cost)** — Place one structure from your player mat to an eligible territory.
- **Enlist (food cost)** — Remove one recruit token and gain its enlist benefit.

#### Action Restrictions
- **One section per turn** — You choose one column, not multiple columns.
- **Same-column pairing** — Top and bottom actions must come from the same selected column.
- **Optionality** — You may skip an action if you cannot/will not execute it.

#### Resource Payment Rules
- **Resource types** — Only food, wood, metal, and oil are resources.
- **Workers are not resources** — Workers cannot be spent as costs.
- **Spend from control** — Spend resources only from territories you control.
- **Cross-territory spending** — You may combine resources from any controlled territories.
- **Spent resources** — Return paid resources to general supply.

#### Unit Capability Snapshot
- **Character** — Moves, transports resources, can fight, can resolve encounters, can gain one Factory card once per game.
- **Mech** — Moves, transports resources, can fight, can carry workers.
- **Worker** — Moves, transports resources, enables produce/deploy/build conditions, does not fight.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Territory and Control
- **Territory types** — Farm, Forest, Mountain, Tundra, Village, Lake, Factory, and Home Base zones are shown on board.
- **Control condition A** — You control a territory if you have at least one unit there.
- **Control condition B** — You control a territory if your structure is there and no enemy units are present.
- **Single controller** — Only one player controls a territory at a time.

#### Home Base Rules
- **Not a territory** — Home base does not count as a territory.
- **Default restriction** — You cannot normally move/build/deploy onto home bases.

#### Resource Persistence
- **Stay on map** — Produced resources remain on territories until moved/spent/lost.
- **Map pressure** — Territory control directly determines usable economy.

#### Encounter and Factory Systems
- **Encounter trigger** — Character can resolve encounter token effects on encounter spaces.
- **Factory reward** — Character can gain one Factory card once per game by ending movement on Factory.
- **Factory scoring note** — Quick-reference excerpt states Factory counts as three territories for end-game scoring context.

#### Achievement Stars
- **Upgrade star** — Complete all 6 upgrades.
- **Deploy star** — Deploy all 4 mechs.
- **Build star** — Build all 4 structures.
- **Enlist star** — Enlist all 4 recruits.
- **Worker star** — Have all 8 workers on board.
- **Objective star** — Reveal 1 completed objective card.
- **Combat stars** — Win combat up to 2 times for stars.
- **Popularity star** — Reach 18 popularity.
- **Power star** — Reach 16 power.

#### Combat Snapshot
- **Combat units** — Plastic units (characters, mechs) engage in combat.
- **Resolution** — Players commit power (and optional combat cards), then compare totals.
- **Retreat** — Loser retreats units to home base.
- **Star gain** — Combat wins can award stars up to the combat-star cap.

#### 2-Player Recruit Rule
--- 2 Players ---
- **Recruit bonus limit** — When your 1 opponent takes a bottom-row action that triggers your Recruit Ongoing Bonus, you gain it only 1 time per trigger (not twice).

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Immediate end** — Game ends the moment any player places a 6th star.

#### Coin Scoring Categories
- **Stars** — Gain coins for each star token placed.
- **Territories** — Gain coins for each territory controlled.
- **Resources** — Gain coins for every set of 2 resources controlled.

#### Popularity Multiplier
- **Track impact** — Your popularity tier determines coin value in all three categories.
- **Higher is better** — More popularity means more coins per scored item.

#### Structure Bonus
- **Bonus tile** — Score additional coins from the selected structure bonus condition.

#### Winner and Tie-Breaks
- **Winner** — Player with the most coins wins.
- **Tie-break 1** — Most territories controlled.
- **Tie-break 2** — Most resources controlled.
- **Tie-break 3** — Most stars placed.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Build a repeatable loop** — Plan your first 3 turns so action-section restriction never forces a dead turn.
- **Upgrade early** — Prioritize one early Upgrade to improve top-row output and lower future bottom-row costs.
- **Expand worker base** — Get workers onto productive terrain fast to unlock resources and later stars.
- **Protect resources** — Keep early resource piles on territories you can hold, not exposed front lines.
- **Track popularity from turn 1** — Popularity multiplies end-game coins, so early neglect is expensive.
- **Use encounters for tempo** — Route your character toward encounter tokens when it doesn’t break your economy loop.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Action tunnel vision** — Picking a strong action now without planning the mandatory different action next turn.
- **Overfighting** — Chasing extra combats after hitting the 2-combat-star cap.
- **Resource overexposure** — Leaving large resource stacks on lightly defended frontier territories.
- **Objective neglect** — Forgetting private objectives until late when they are harder to pivot into.
- **Popularity neglect** — Building a strong board but finishing in a weak popularity tier.
- **Bad end timing** — Triggering the 6th-star end while behind in territory/resources/popularity conversion.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Race vs value** — Decide whether to rush six stars or delay endgame for stronger coin conversion.
- **Engine vs map** — Decide when to spend turns on upgrades/enlists versus claiming/holding key territories.
- **Threat vs spend** — Decide whether to keep power as deterrence or spend it to secure pivotal combats.
- **Factory timing** — Decide whether to divert for Factory card now or maintain local economic tempo first.

metadata.notes: Primary source used: provided Scythe rulebook excerpt and quick reference text. Reformatted for scanability with explicit player-count audit headers.
